site.studioTagline: Worlds that only work because they shouldn't.
site.gameTagline: Chicago, 1929. The night is yours to organize.

hero.title: Improbability Works
hero.tagline: Worlds that only work because they shouldn't.
hero.body: Independent studio crafting cooperative experiences where elegance, chaos, and human organization collide.

manifesto.title: Five convictions. No asterisks.
manifesto.intro: What the studio actually believes. Not a style guide — a set of refusals.

pillars.title: Independence, not isolation
pillars.body: Being independent means answering to the work, not a quarterly target. Traditional publisher pressure — milestone gates, forced timelines, genre mandates — is a no. Collaboration with publishers who genuinely respect the project is something else. Independence is about keeping the thread, not refusing every helping hand.

pillars.title: Systems, not scripts
pillars.body: We build games the way you plant a garden, not the way you write a play. Interesting things emerge from systems in collision. Scripts close down possibilities. Systems multiply them.

pillars.title: Better with someone else
pillars.body: The most interesting things happen when humans must coordinate, negotiate, and improvise together under pressure. Not a dogma about form — cooperative, competitive, asymmetric — the game decides. But the territory worth exploring is almost always human organization at its most fragile.

pillars.title: Earned discovery
pillars.body: A well-built system teaches itself through what it is. Consequences follow from actions. The interface reflects the reality. You learn at whatever pace you choose — there's no single right way through a game that trusts you to find your own. Tutorials aren't the problem. Condescension is.

pillars.title: The experience is the point
pillars.body: Not genre exercises. Every game should deliver what only games can: fun that only makes sense once you've had it, a story that belongs to the people who played it, a moment worth keeping. Mechanics are vocabulary, not purpose. A mechanic you can't feel has no business being there.

roadmap.title: Built in the open, at an honest pace.
roadmap.intro: No ship dates we don't mean. Follow along on Discord for week-by-week devlog.

stages.label: Now
stages.title: Vertical slice in development
stages.body: One night, one speakeasy, all core systems wired end-to-end.

stages.label: Next
stages.title: Closed playtest (Valve Playtest)
stages.body: Small, invite-only. We want tables of friends, not strangers.

stages.label: Soon
stages.title: Steam Early Access
stages.body: Because the game is about your table's stories, it needs tables.

stages.label: Later
stages.title: Full release
stages.body: When the evenings are worth remembering without us explaining why.

team.title: A studio of one, for now.
team.intro: Small on purpose. Slower on purpose. Accountable on purpose.

member.name: Will
member.bio: Solo developer building Velvet Door. Blueprint-first, coffee-driven, obsessed with systems that tell stories. Based in France.

newsletter.title: Major updates only, delivered when they matter.
newsletter.body: Low traffic. Major milestones and closed playtest invites. No spam, ever.
newsletter.consent: I agree to receive occasional emails from Improbability Works about Velvet Door and studio news. I can unsubscribe at any time.
